title: ctenitis mayottensis
intro: Ctenitis mayottensis, also known as Mayotte's fern, is a species of fern native to the Comoros Islands in the Indian Ocean. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Ctenitis mayottensis is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, and is also dried for later use in folk remedies.
